---
title: ""
---

:::{.hero-section}
# Acyclic by Design

:::{.lead style="font-size: 1.4rem; font-weight: 300; max-width: 800px; margin-bottom: 2rem;"}
First Synthesis LLC engineers absolute mathematical boundaries for experimental logic and deterministic execution. Our initial technology release features a powerful dual architecture: the **Monist Engine**, a high-performance GPU paradox evaluator, and its formal companion lab, **NF-Sketches**, a deep embedding in Lean 4 verifying its theoretical bounds.
:::

:::{.action-buttons style="margin-bottom: 4rem;"}
<a href="monist.qmd" class="btn-primary">Explore the Hardware</a>
<a href="nf-sketches.qmd" class="btn-secondary">Explore the Mathematics</a>
:::
:::

## The 5-Layer Execution Pipeline

We map logical intent into physical memory across an unbroken pipeline from the semantic boundary down to VRAM.

:::{.grid style="margin-top: 3rem; gap: 2rem;"}

:::{.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 style="padding: 1.5rem; background: #fdfdfd; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #eaeaea;"}
### 1. The Human Interface
Users write high-level constraints via natural deduction in the `FormulaArena`. Instead of conditional logic, intent is mapped instantly into discrete spatial relationships.
:::

:::{.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 style="padding: 1.5rem; background: #fdfdfd; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #eaeaea;"}
### 2. The Geometry Layer
The CPU converts text-based logic into a spatial map. It mathematically simplifies redundant equations and actively scans the structure to catch paradoxical loops before they can execute.
:::

:::{.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 style="padding: 1.5rem; background: #fdfdfd; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #eaeaea;"}
### 3. Graph Reduction
We completely destroy the variable. The compiler leverages De Bruijn indexing to transform syntax into nameless, untyped S, K, I combinators.
:::

:::{.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 style="padding: 1.5rem; background: #fdfdfd; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #eaeaea;"}
### 4. The Physics Engine
Lock-free WGSL compute shaders collide atomic nodes in massive parallel swarms inside GPU VRAM, unburdened by Garbage Collection.
:::

:::{.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 style="padding: 1.5rem; background: #fdfdfd; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #eaeaea;"}
### 5. Holographic Co-processor
We use Vector Symbolic Architectures (HDC) to superpose arrays into a continuous wave function, canceling logical noise in $O(1)$ time.
:::

:::
